differential_diagnoses: Severe insulin resistance syndrome (Type B insulin resistance)
next_steps: Test for anti-insulin receptor antibodies to rule out Type B insulin resistance
Perform insulin-to-C-peptide ratio assessment during a supervised glucose challenge
Check serum leptin levels and re-evaluate total body composition/fat distribution
Review medication adherence via serum insulin and C-peptide levels in a controlled setting
Screen for autoimmune diseases, including Sjogren’s syndrome and other connective tissue disorders
Consult endocrinology, dermatology, and immunology specialists
Initiate leptin replacement therapy if indicated and approved

differential_diagnoses: Factitious disorder (insulin nonadherence or metabolic manipulation)
Severe insulin resistance due to autoimmune syndrome
Paraneoplastic cachexia with secondary insulin resistance
Lipodystrophy associated with systemic autoimmune disease (e.g., lupus-associated)
next_steps: Obtain random serum insulin and C-peptide levels during hyperglycemic state to assess exogenous versus endogenous status
Conduct a formal psychiatric evaluation for factitious disorder and eating disorders
Evaluate insulin injection sites via ultrasound to exclude subcutaneous insulin sequestration
Repeat ANA, anti-dsDNA, and extend workup for systemic autoimmune markers (e.g., complement components, specific autoantibodies)
Perform a supervised fasting test if safety allows to assess for true insulin resistance versus nonadherence
Re-evaluate abdominal pain with a focus on autoimmune pancreatitis or secondary GI issues
